input.background: The following data were drawn one observation at a time in the order records: b gb g b g. Here, $b$ denotes a defective item and $g$ denotes a good item. The experiment was performed to test the hypothesis: $$ \mathrm{H}_0: \mathrm{p}=\mathrm{p}_0=0.10 \text { versus } \mathrm{H}_1: \mathrm{p}=\mathrm{p}_1=0.25 . $$ where $p$ denotes the proportion of defective items in the population. It is desired to reject $\mathrm{H}_0$ when it is true with probability 0.05 and to accept $\mathrm{H}_0$ when $\mathrm{H}_1$ is true with probability 0.10.
input.question: Using the sequential testing plan, determine whether you would accept or reject a lot on the basis of the observations: b gb g b g.

output.answer: The sampling is stopped at the $6^{\text {th }}$ observation and that the lot would be rejected.
